article_title: What Would Living on The Moon Really Do to The Human Body?
summary_v1: The US is planning to send humans to the Moon to live and work for extended periods of time. This will mark a new era of space exploration since the Apollo missions. Scientists are concerned about how living on the Moon would affect the human body. Long-term exposure to low gravity, radiation, and isolation could have unknown consequences. Further research is needed to understand these effects before long-duration lunar missions can be safely conducted.
summary_v2: NASA and private space companies are planning to send humans to live on the Moon for extended periods, with missions lasting up to a year or more. Scientists are concerned about the effects of long-term space travel on the human body, including radiation exposure and muscle loss in microgravity environments. This new era of lunar exploration is set to begin soon, marking a significant milestone in deep-space research.

article_title: NextDC issues $1 billion 100-year bond to fund AI infrastructure
summary_v1: NextDC has issued a $1 billion 100-year bond to finance its expansion plans. The bond is backed by Canadian pension fund La Caisse. The funding will be used for the construction of new data centers. This follows NextDC's recent announcement of a separate fundraising effort. The bond marks a significant milestone in NextDC's growth plans.
summary_v2: NextDC has issued a $1 billion, 100-year bond with backing from Canadian pension giant La Caisse to fund its expansion into AI infrastructure. The ASX-listed digital infrastructure operator will use the financing to build data centers. The deal is one of the longest-term bonds ever issued by an Australian company.

article_title: Anthropic Number One AI in Ranking and Revenue – Making $30 Billion Per Year
summary_v1: Anthropic has secured a deal with Google and Broadcom for next-generation computing capacity. The agreement will provide Anthropic with "multiple gigawatts" of TPUs, which are expected to come online by 2027. This expansion is aimed at powering Anthropic's "frontier Claude models". Anthropic has previously been ranked as the number one AI in terms of revenue and ranking. As a result of this deal, the company will continue to scale its operations.
summary_v2: Anthropic, ranked number one in AI ranking and revenue, at $30 billion per year, has signed agreements with Google and Broadcom for new high-capacity computing infrastructure expected to go live in 2027. The agreement will provide multiple gigawatts of TPU (Tensor Processing Unit) capacity to power Anthropic's advanced AI models. This expansion is a significant step forward for the company's operations and growth.
technical_terms: TPU: A Tensor Processing Unit, or TPU, is a specialized processor designed specifically to accelerate machine learning and artificial intelligence computations. It can perform complex matrix operations much faster than general-purpose CPUs. | GPU: GPUs (Graphics Processing Units) are computer chips that were originally designed for graphics rendering but are now widely used for accelerating complex mathematical computations, including those in deep learning and AI. They can handle many tasks simultaneously due to their parallel architecture. | Tensor Processing Unit: A Tensor Processing Unit is a dedicated processor for efficiently processing large datasets of numerical data, particularly in the context of machine learning and artificial intelligence.

article_title: Firms take on AI chatbots in visibility fight
summary_v1: Indian companies may challenge AI chatbots in court due to concerns that they divert users away from company websites without explanation. This could lead to a loss of traffic and business for the firms. The issue is related to AI-generated responses that exclude certain websites and users without providing any reason or recourse. Businesses are preparing for a courtroom battle over this issue. The use of AI platforms has changed how users discover information online, raising concerns about transparency.
summary_v2: Companies in India may take legal action against AI chatbots for diverting users away from their websites and excluding them from AI-generated responses without explanation. This has raised concerns among Indian businesses about lost traffic and transparency issues. The shift towards AI platforms is altering online information discovery, sparking a potential "visibility fight" between companies and AI providers.

article_title: Tech firms take foot off hiring pedal on AI turn
summary_v1: India's tech sector has seen a decline in active job openings, from 256,000 in April 2022 to 110,000 currently. This represents an 8% drop. The decline is attributed to companies shedding staff due to the increasing use of artificial intelligence (AI). As a result, AI is taking over roles that were previously handled by humans. Tech firms had accounted for 83% of all hiring in the country prior to the downturn.
summary_v2: India's tech sector active job openings dropped by 8% to 110,000 in April 2022 compared to the previous year, as companies such as those in IT services begin to shed staff due to increasing use of artificial intelligence. The decline is significant given that the tech industry accounted for 83% of all hiring in India just a year ago. This shift marks a reversal from the post-pandemic surge that had created 256,000 active job openings in April 2021.
technical_terms: artificial intelligence: A field of computer science focused on creating intelligent machines that can perform tasks requiring human-like reasoning and problem-solving skills.

article_title: Tech job openings fall 8% in April as AI replaces headcount
summary_v1: India's tech sector saw an 8% drop in active job openings in April compared to last year. The number of open positions fell from 256,000 to 110,000. Tech accounted for 83% of all hiring in India before this decline.
summary_v2: Tech job openings in India dropped by 8% in April to 110,000, a significant decline from the peak of 256,000 active openings in April 2022, as AI replaces human headcount and companies shed staff. This shift is part of a broader trend where automation is taking over roles that humans previously handled. The tech sector still accounts for 83% of all hiring in India.

article_title: OpenAI urges California, Delaware to investigate Musk's 'anti-competitive behaviour'
summary_v1: OpenAI has asked California and Delaware to investigate Elon Musk's actions, alleging "anti-competitive behaviour". This comes from a lawsuit filed by Musk in 2024 against OpenAI and its CEO Sam Altman. The suit claims they are violating OpenAI's founding mission as it shifts to a for-profit entity. Musk was a co-founder of OpenAI in 2015 but left two years later. A trial between the two sides is set to take place, with the request for an investigation made ahead of this date.
summary_v2: OpenAI has asked California and Delaware's attorneys general to investigate Elon Musk's alleged "anti-competitive behavior" amid an ongoing lawsuit. The company accuses Musk, its former cofounder, of improperly influencing OpenAI's restructuring as it transitions from a non-profit to a for-profit entity. The investigation request is made ahead of the trial between OpenAI and Musk in 2024.

article_title: Samsung flags eightfold jump in Q1 profit as AI chip demand drives up prices
summary_v1: Samsung estimated an operating profit of $37.92 billion for Q1, exceeding forecasts and last year's result by over 8 times. The company cited strong demand for AI chips driving the increase. This is a record-high profit for Samsung's first quarter. The projected figure is significantly higher than analyst estimates. The profit jump comes from booming demand for artificial intelligence infrastructure.
summary_v2: Samsung estimated an operating profit of $37.92 billion for Q1, an eight-fold increase from last year, driven by high demand for AI chips. This surpasses analyst estimates of 40.6 trillion won ($33.7 billion). The record-high profit is due to booming demand for artificial intelligence infrastructure.
technical_terms: AI: Artificial Intelligence refers to computer systems that can perform tasks that typically require human intelligence, such as learning and problem-solving. | AI chips: Specialized computer chips designed specifically for artificial intelligence tasks, often including hardware accelerators to speed up processing. | Artificial Intelligence infrastructure: The underlying systems and hardware that support the operation of AI models and applications, such as servers, storage, and networking equipment.
technical_term_names: AI, AI chips, Artificial Intelligence infrastructure

article_title: With Vera, Nvidia stops borrowing and starts building its own CPU muscle
summary_v1: Nvidia is shifting from relying on external CPU suppliers to developing its own CPUs. This move is part of CEO Jensen Huang's plan to create an "AI factory" with tightly controlled development processes. The company showcased its self-developed next-generation CPU at GTC 2026. Artificial General Intelligence (AGI) relies heavily on CPU performance, making this shift strategic for Nvidia. By developing its own CPUs, Nvidia aims to improve efficiency and control in its AI operations.
summary_v2: Nvidia has announced it will stop relying on external CPU development and instead build its own CPUs with its new "Vera" technology. This move aims to create a tightly controlled "AI factory" for artificial general intelligence (AGI) applications. The company showcased Vera at GTC 2026, highlighting its potential to revolutionize AI computing.
technical_terms: Vera: Nvidia's new technology aimed at building its own CPUs for AI and other applications. | AI factory: A system that tightly controls all components to optimize performance and efficiency in AI computations, often associated with artificial general intelligence (AGI) tasks. | AGI: Artificial General Intelligence is a type of AI that can perform any intellectual task that humans can. It's still an emerging concept, aiming to be more advanced than narrow or specialized AIs.
